mnemonics: ACF PACF Party: ACF is the 'All-inclusive' Correlation Function (total effect), while PACF is the 'Private' AutoCorrelation Function (direct effect only).
keyPoints: For an AR(p) model, the ACF decays gradually while the PACF cuts off after lag p.
For an MA(q) model, the ACF cuts off after lag q while the PACF decays gradually.
Stationarity implies that the autocorrelation function (ACF) depends only on the lag, not on the absolute time point.
commonMistakes: Assuming a significant autocorrelation at a high lag implies a direct relationship, when it might just be a carry-over effect from earlier lags (confusing ACF and PACF).
Forgetting that stationarity is a prerequisite for these standard autocorrelation properties and trying to interpret the ACF/PACF of a non-stationary series.
